title: Bridge Essay: Distinct prefrontal projection activity and transcriptional state conversely orc

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Wind/Pride** (Phase 4) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Wind/Pride to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Distinct prefrontal projection activity and transcriptional state conversely orchestrate social competition and hierarchy.** — Neuron. PMID: 38086372. DOI: 10.1016/j.neuron.2023.11.012. Social animals compete for limited resources, resulting in asocial hierarchy. Although different neuronal subpopulations in the medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC), which has been mechanistically implicated in social dominance behavior, encode distinct social competition behaviors, their identities and associated molecular underpinnings have not yet been identified. In this study, we found that mPFC neurons projecting to the nucleus accumbens (mPFC-NAc) encode social winning behavior, whereas mPFC
